comments.comment: The release of copper at CFB Borden primarily occurs from small arms ranges and explosives training activities.  A large portion is recovered as small arms brass which is sent off-site and recycled.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: The release of copper at CFB Borden primarily occurs from small arms ranges and explosives training activities.  A large portion is recovered as brass which is recycled.  Previous annual submissions were calculated using data tables within Canadian Forces Range Information System (CFRIS).  Releases for 2024 were calculated using updated small arms bullet and casing compositions.  2024 also reported 14,200 kg of brass recycling to an off-site Canadian Forces Ammunition Depot which was not present in last years submission.

comments.comment: Transfer of brass casings was from the Ranges to CFAD Angus for processing and storage.

type.fr: Commentaire sur le recyclage

comments.comment: Brass casings from CFB Borden CFAD were sent to Dundurn CFAD for recycling.
